revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:登極令]]
{{Laws and Regulations of Japan |題名 = {{ill|Imperial Accession Order|ja|登極令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Accession Order|qq=}} |効力 = 廃止 |種類 = {{ill|Constitutional law|en|Constitutional law|qid=Q11206|lt=Constitution|1=Constitutional law}} |内容 = {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor of Japan|1=Emperor of Japan}}の{{ill|Senso|ja|践祚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senso|1=senso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4504500}}{{ill|Sokui|ja|即位礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 即位の礼|1=Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4202178}} |関連 = {{ill|Imperial House Law (1889)|ja|皇室典範 (1889th year )|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial House Law (1889)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |リンク = [{{NDLDC|2951035/1}} 官報 1909th year 2月11日] |ウィキソース = {{ill|Imperial Accession Order|ja|登極令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Accession Order|qq=}} }}
The '''{{ill|Imperial Accession Order|ja|登極令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Accession Order|qq=}}''' (とうきょくれい), formally issued as '''Imperial Household Order No. 1 of February 11, 1909''' (明治42nd year 2月11日皇室令第1号), was an '''Imperial Household Order''' under the {{ill|Meiji Constitution|en|Meiji Constitution|qid=Q122804|lt=Meiji Constitution|1=Meiji Constitution}} and the {{ill|Imperial House Law (1889)|ja|旧皇室典範|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial House Law (1889)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皇室典範 (1889年)|1=Imperial Household Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581033}}, regulating the procedures for the '''{{ill|Senso|ja|践祚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senso|1=senso|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4504500}}''', '''{{ill|Sokui|ja|即位礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sokui|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 即位の礼|1=Enthronement of the Japanese emperor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4202178}}''' (Enthronement Ceremony), '''{{ill|Daijosai|en|Daijosai|qid=Q11433653|lt=Daijosai|1=Daijosai}}''' (Great Thanksgiving Festival), and '''{{ill|Gengō|ja|元号|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gengō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Era name|qid=Q256408}}''' (Era Name).<ref name="nihonkokugo">[https://kotobank.jp/word/%E7%99%BB%E6%A5%B5%E4%BB%A4-579989#E7.B2.BE.E9.81.B8.E7.89.88.20.E6.97.A5.E6.9C.AC.E5.9B.BD.E8.AA.9E.E5.A4.A7.E8.BE.9E.E5.85.B8 精選版 日本国語大辞典 - Kotobank]</ref> It was promulgated on '''February 11, 1909'''.
The order was repealed on '''May 2, 1947''', the day before the implementation of the '''{{ill|Constitution of Japan|en|Constitution of Japan|qid=Q237082|lt=Constitution of Japan|1=Constitution of Japan}}''' and the '''{{ill|Imperial Household Law|en|Imperial Household Law|qid=Q1324733|lt=Imperial House Law|1=Imperial Household Law}}''', by the "Imperial Household Order and Associated Laws Abolishment Order" (昭和22nd year 皇室令第12号).
== Overview == The '''Imperial Accession Order''' consisted of '''18 articles''' and two '''appendices'''.<ref name="nihonkokugo"/>
The provisions included: * Upon '''Senso''' (ascension), the '''Chief of the Bureau of Shinto Affairs''' (掌典長) was to conduct a ritual at the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}, reporting the accession to the '''Imperial Ancestral Shrine''' (皇霊殿) and the '''Shrine of the Gods''' (神殿). * Immediately after '''Senso''', the era name ('''Gengō''') must be changed ('''Ichisei Ichigen''' system 一世一元の制). * The new era name was determined by '''Imperial Edict''' ('''Chokutei''') following consultation with the '''Privy Council'''. * The '''Sokui no Rei''' (Enthronement Ceremony) and '''Daijōsai''' (Great Thanksgiving Festival) were to be conducted in autumn or winter, and an '''Imperial Grand Steward's Office''' ('''Daireishi''') was to be established to oversee the ceremonies. * The dates for '''Sokui no Rei''' and '''Daijōsai''' were to be announced by '''Imperial Household Minister''' (宮内大臣) and '''State Ministers''' (国務大臣). * Before conducting these ceremonies, '''Imperial Envoys''' were to be dispatched to the '''Ise Grand Shrine''' (伊勢神宮), the '''Mausoleum of Emperor Jimmu''' (神武天皇山陵), and the mausolea of the four previous emperors to offer '''heihaku''' (offerings). * The '''Daijōsai''' involved selecting two '''sacred rice fields''': the '''Yuki Province''' (悠紀地方) (east/south of Kyoto) and the '''Suki Province''' (主基地方) (west/north of Kyoto), chosen by '''Imperial Edict'''. * Before the '''Sokui no Rei''', the emperor must '''relocate to Kyoto''' with the empress, bringing the '''Imperial Regalia of Japan''' (三種の神器). * On the day of '''Sokui no Rei''', imperial envoys must report the event to the '''Imperial Ancestral Shrine''' (皇霊殿) and the '''Shrine of the Gods''' (神殿). * On the day of '''Daijōsai''', imperial envoys must offer '''heihaku''' at the '''Ise Grand Shrine''', the '''Imperial Ancestral Shrine''', the '''Shrine of the Gods''', and all '''Kangō Shrines''' (官幣大社, 国幣大社). * The '''Chinkonsai''' (鎮魂祭) was to be held one day before '''Daijōsai'''. * After the '''Sokui no Rei''' and '''Daijōsai''', the '''Grand Banquet''' ('''Daikyō''') would be held, and the emperor and empress would personally visit the '''Ise Grand Shrine''', '''Emperor Jimmu’s Mausoleum''', and the mausolea of the last four emperors. * Upon returning to '''Tokyo Imperial Palace''', the emperor and empress would perform '''an audience at the Imperial Ancestral Shrine'''. * During periods of '''Ryoan''' (mourning for the previous emperor), the '''Sokui no Rei''' and '''Daijōsai''' were '''not to be conducted'''.
The '''appendices''' provided '''detailed procedural regulations''' for each of these ceremonies.
== References == <references />
{{law-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|登極令|version=99056330|comment=Imported full ja history}}
